summary: A strong audience (AUD) recording captures a solid 1982 Spectrum show, highlighted by a standout Friend of the Devil and an epic Bertha → Playin' → Ship jam.
review: The audience tape is praised as clear and well‑balanced, though it suffers from occasional crowd noise and a few missing seconds at the start of the second set. Musically the concert is solid – first‑set workmanlike with a superb Friend of the Devil, while the second set delivers a memorable Bertha>Playin' into Ship of Fools jam, even if some listeners felt the later second‑set moments were a bit wacky. Overall the performance is enjoyable with several high points despite minor slip‑ups.

ai_rating.rationale: Multiple detailed reviews give a consistent picture of good musical performance and a clear audience recording; occasional criticisms keep the rating just below excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Brilliant, crisp audience tape with good balance; some missing seconds at the start of the second set, a fast patch in Jack Straw, and occasional crowd interruptions (yelling during Playin' jam).

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and tight; the second set launches into a legendary Bertha → Playin' → Ship of Fools sequence with an extended post‑Ship jam into Drums, then continues with a wilder second Playin', Good Lovin' (less inspired) and a perfect Dough Knees.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is praised as ‘great’ and his guitar work described as firing on eight cylinders; tone noted as excellent.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned in the context of swapping places with Jerry for the drummers' segment; no specific bass commentary.
band_member_comments.Drums: The post‑Ship jam into Drums is highlighted as a unique, extended excursion with both drummers staying in the mix.
